channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Polanco hits winning single in the 15th inning as the Mariners beat the Tigers to advance to ALCS
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Jorge Polanco hit a game-ending single in the 15th inning, and the Seattle Mariners advanced to the AL Championship Series by outlasting the Detroit Tigers for a 3-2 victory Friday night in the longest winner-take-all postseason game in baseball history.
item.dc:creator: LUKE OLSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tarik Skubal turns in a dominant performance in Detroit's Game 5 loss to Mariners
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Tarik Skubal was terrific. And he was long gone when the season ended for the Detroit Tigers. Skubal struck out 13 in six dominant innings in Game 5 of the AL Division Series at Seattle on Friday night. But the Tigers were eliminated when Jorge Polanco singled home J.P.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Balogun's second-half goal gives US a 1-1 tie against Ecuador in friendly
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Folarin Balogun scored in the 71st minute, offsetting Enner Valencia's first-half goal, and the United States tied Ecuador 1-1 in a friendly on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Padres' Iglesias suspended 1 game, Bogaerts fined for dispute with umpires after season-ending loss
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — San Diego Padres infielder Jose Iglesias was suspended Friday for one game and fined for unprofessional conduct toward the umpires during the final game of the club’s wild-card playoff series against the Chicago Cubs, MLB announced.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Celtics star Paul Pierce found asleep in car, arrested on suspicion of DUI
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former NBA player Paul Pierce was arrested Tuesday night on suspicion of driving under the influence on a Los Angeles highway after he was found asleep behind the wheel, state police said. California Highway Patrol officers responded at about 10:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clemson's Howard's Rock, 'Play Like a Champion Today' signs among beloved totems in college football
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Hunter Renfrow snagged the game-winning touchdown in the College Football Playoff national championship game, was selected in the fifth round of the NFL draft and even made the Pro Bowl.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Manchester is saying its last goodbye to boxing icon Ricky Hatton
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Manchester is saying its last goodbye to boxing icon Ricky Hatton. The former world champion died last month aged 46. His funeral was being held Friday in the city’s cathedral and fans lined up as the cortège made its way through the streets.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Parity or poor football? NFL is providing plenty of close games with exciting finishes
item.description: Some call it parity. Others consider it bad football. No matter how it’s viewed, the NFL is providing plenty of close games, thrilling comebacks, big upsets and late-minute heroics. It keeps viewers entertained — or at least tuned in until the end.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matthew Boyd stars as the Cubs blank the Brewers 6-0 to send the NLDS to a deciding Game 5
item.description: Matthew Boyd pitched two-hit ball into the fifth inning, and the Chicago Cubs shut down the Milwaukee Brewers for a 6-0 victory Thursday night that pushed their NL Division Series all the way to a decisive Game 5.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Excitement builds for Wrigleyville businesses ahead of Game 4 of NLDS
item.description: It's all on the line for the Cubs once again as Game 4 of the NLDS inches closer, and excitement is starting to build around Wrigley Field, in bars and other area businesses.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: Dolphins' Tua Tagovailoa explains how he tunes out noise and criticism
item.description: Miami Dolphins quarterback Tua Tagovailoa said he tunes out the noise and criticism by avoiding social media and turning to his family, especially his kids.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WNBA star Angel Reese breaks barriers as the first athlete to walk the Victoria's Secret runway
item.description: Angel Reese's impact has expanded to the runway, as the Chicago Sky star has been selected as the first athlete in history to walk in the Victoria’s Secret Fashion Show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Past, present & future: 50 years of Buccaneers football
item.description: There's a saying: teamwork makes the dream work. And countless people have poured into the Tampa Bay Buccaneers to make the franchise as successful as it is today.
item.dc:creator: Will Richardson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NFL fines Jerry Jones $250,000 for obscene MetLife gesture he says was inadvertent
item.description: FRISCO, Texas (AP) — The NFL said Tuesday it has fined Jerry Jones $250,000 for an obscene gesture toward fans at MetLife Stadium that the Dallas owner said was “inadvertent” and intended for fans of the Cowboys, not the New York Jets. The incident came late in Dallas' 37-22 victory on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James teased 'the decision of all decisions' announcement. It was an ad
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James had another “decision” to announce. Turns out, it was an ad. The Los Angeles Lakers star teased that he would have something to say Tuesday. The “decision” reference is a nod to how he announced in July 2010 that he was joining the Miami Heat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New owners of Tampa Bay Rays share excitement, optimism for team's future
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. - All eyes were on the Tampa Bay Rays this morning as the new owners took to Steinbrenner Field to lay out their vision for the future.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Bass, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mammoth GM hopes to sign Logan Cooley to long-term deal
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - After four years of rebuilding, if the Utah Mammoth don't make the NHL playoffs this season, would it be a disappointment? "Yes, I think we would," said general manager Bill Armstrong.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Cubs drop Game 2 at Brewers, head back to Chicago on the brink of elimination
item.description: MILWAUKEE, Wisc. (WGN) — Monday night started off with so much Seiya Suzuki-fueled promise, but the Milwaukee Brewers' lineup was once more too powerful for the Chicago Cubs pitching staff to overcome.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bartender rendered aid to Mark Sanchez in Indianapolis stabbing
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — Scott Bennett was closing up Loughmiller’s Pub & Eatery at 12:30 a.m. Saturday when an injured man started pounding on the bar’s window. "As I was closing, somebody was beating on the window, just a random person," Bennett said.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez stabbed multiple times in altercation leading to charges against him
item.description: Former NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez was pepper-sprayed and stabbed multiple times during a late-night altercation with a 69-year-old truck driver in a downtown Indianapolis alley, which resulted in criminal charges against the Fox Sports analyst, according to court records filed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MIKE MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 49ers' Christian McCaffrey call San Francisco 'San Fran' in new commercial
item.description: (KRON) — 49ers running back and former Stanford University standout Christian McCaffrey committed a Bay Area cultural faux pas in a new Lowe’s ad posted to social media on Oct. 1. “What’s up, San Fran?” Christian McCaffrey says in the advertisement.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: RSL moves back above playoff line with 1-0 victory
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - In its final regular season home game of the year, Real Salt Lake came up with its biggest victory of the season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Fast start propels Brewers to 9-3 victory over Cubs in NLDS opener
item.description: Chourio capped Milwaukee's six-run first inning with a two-run single, and the Brewers trounced the Chicago Cubs 9-3 on Saturday in Game 1 of their NL Division Series.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma WR Keontez Lewis leaves game after headfirst collision with brick barrier
item.description: Oklahoma receiver Keontez Lewis left Saturday's game against Kent State in the first quarter after hitting his head on a brick barrier following an attempt at a catch.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is the baseball you caught at an MLB game worth anything? How to know
item.description: Catching a ball - whether a record-setting home run or a foul ball tossed into the stands - can be exhilarating.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9
